value_proposition: Issue green receipts, track income, and stay compliant with Portuguese tax law — in plain English.
one_liner: The English-friendly recibos verdes tool for digital nomads and expats in Portugal.

customer.primary: English-speaking digital nomads and expats on D8 visas in Portugal — primarily Lisbon, Porto, Algarve
customer.secondary: Portuguese-speaking freelancers (trabalhadores independentes) who find Portal das Finanças UX outdated
customer.pain: Must navigate a Portuguese-only government portal (AT/Portal das Finanças) with no English support, no guidance, no automation — and they're hiring accountants at €30-80/month just to emit receipts
current_workarounds: Struggling with Portal das Finanças directly
Paying Rauva €128/month for a bundled accountant
verbatim_pain_language: How do I issue green receipts in Portugal?
Portal das Finanças is all in Portuguese — I have no idea what I'm doing
My accountant charges me €80/month just to do my recibos verdes
I don't understand if I need to pay IRS quarterly or annually
What is this 'retenção na fonte' field?

market.regulatory_context: AT (Autoridade Tributária e Aduaneira) mandates — paper receipts abolished Jan 2024, B2G e-invoicing Jan 2025, QES requirement Jan 2026, NHR 2.0 active

competitors.name: ATGO (official AT app)
competitors.threat_level: low — it's the pain point we solve, not a real competitor

competitors.name: InvoiceXpress
weaknesses: Per-document pricing confusing for irregular freelancers

competitors.name: Cegid Jasmin
weaknesses: No AT portal direct workflow guidance
competitors.threat_level: medium — free tier is pricing pressure

competitors.name: Rauva
weaknesses: Targets Lda. companies more than trabalhadores independentes
Expensive for simple recibos verdes use

unfair_advantage: English-first and expat-native — purpose-built for the D8 visa + NIF registration → first recibo verde journey that no competitor serves. Every competitor is a Portuguese product adapted for English speakers; VerdeDesk is an English product built on Portuguese tax rails.

monetisation.rationale: Accountants charge €30-80/month for the same workflow. At €9/month we are 3-8x cheaper than the incumbent solution. Jasmin free tier is the main pricing pressure — must win on English UX and guided onboarding.
monetisation.freemium_verdict: Not justified at this stage. Free tier creates support burden without driving acquisition. The trial converts better for a tool with a clear compliance job-to-be-done.

communities.name: Portugalist
communities.notes: Portugal expat guide with high search traffic

communities.name: Digital Nomads & Entrepreneurs in Portugal, Madeira & Azores

market_timing.impact: All freelancers must now use electronic tools — demand for good software is mandatory
New compliance layer; freelancers working with government clients need structured e-invoicing
New complexity; freelancers need to understand and implement QES — guidance opportunity
Expat tax incentive restructured; correct categorisation more consequential than before
Steady inflow of new English-speaking freelancers who need this from Day 1
Caused fresh confusion even for experienced users — new onboarding need

business_signals.stage_notes: Pre-validation. Landing page not yet deployed. No signal data available.
